2026-04-20T17:30:22.6422193Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6422747Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6423187Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6423556Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6424049Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6424280Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6424435Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6424613Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6424776Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6425001Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6425195Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6425488Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T17:30:22.6425763Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

{"timestamp":"2026-04-20T17:33:47.500704183Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.SdkAnalyzers`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\n**Triggered by:** commit f74b0b25c (PR #58154) by @m-redding\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58154 added:\n- **AZC0101** analyzer: detects `ConfigureAwait(true)` in Azure SDK library code and warns to use `ConfigureAwait(false)` instead\n- **ConfigureAwaitCodeFixProvider**: automatic code fixer that replaces `ConfigureAwait(true)` → `ConfigureAwait(false)`\n- Shared async infrastructure: `AsyncPatternAnalyzer`, `MethodBodyAnalyzer`, `AsyncAnalyzerUtilities`\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG.md** lists only `AZC0012` in Features Added — does not mention the newly-added `AZC0101` rule, its code fixer, or the shared async pattern utilities introduced in this push\n- **README.md** is missing the required top-level sections: *Getting started* (install, prerequisites, authenticate), *Key concepts*, *Examples*, *Troubleshooting*, *Next steps*, and *Contributing*\n- **README.md** does not describe how library authors can suppress AZC0101 (or other rules), nor does it provide a quick usage example showing what the analyzer flags\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### 1a. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/CHANGELOG.md` — edit\n\nAdd an entry for AZC0101 and the code fixer under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`:\n\n```markdown\n### Features Added\n\n- Initial release of Azure.SdkAnalyzers\n- Added AZC0012: Avoid single word type names analyzer\n- Added AZC0020: Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext analyzer\n- Added AZC0101: Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` analyzer with automatic code fix (replaces `true` with `false`)\n```\n\n#### 1b. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` — edit\n\nReplace the current README content with a fully structured document matching the canonical template. The README currently has a valid intro and rules table; keep those and add the missing sections around them:\n\n```markdown\n# Azure SDK Analyzers\n\nRoslyn analyzers that enforce [Azure SDK Design Guidelines](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html) for .NET library authors.\n\nThis package is automatically included in all Azure SDK libraries in this repository to ensure consistent code quality and adherence to Azure SDK conventions.\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` NuGet package via the .NET CLI:\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.SdkAnalyzers\n```\n\nOr via the NuGet Package Manager:\n\n```powershell\nInstall-Package Azure.SdkAnalyzers\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- .NET SDK 6.0 or later\n- A project that targets any of the supported Azure SDK target frameworks\n\n### Authenticate the client\n\nThis package contains build-time analyzers only. No authentication is required.\n\n## Key concepts\n\n`Azure.SdkAnalyzers` provides Roslyn (compile-time) analyzers and code fixers that enforce patterns required by the [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html). Diagnostics fire as warnings during `dotnet build` and are visible in the IDE editor.\n\nFor the full list of rules, see [docs/list-of-diagnostics.md](docs/list-of-diagnostics.md).\n\n## Implemented Rules\n\n| Rule | Description | Fix |\n|------|-------------|-----|\n| **AZC0012** | Avoid single word type names | — |\n| **AZC0020** | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — |\n| **AZC0101** | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ |\n\n## Examples\n\n### AZC0101 — ConfigureAwait(false)\n\nThe analyzer flags any `await` that passes `true` to `ConfigureAwait`:\n\n```C# Snippet:AZC0101Example\n// ❌ AZC0101 warning — ConfigureAwait(true) is almost always wrong in library code\nawait task.ConfigureAwait(true);\n\n// ✅ Correct — use false to avoid capturing the caller's SynchronizationContext\nawait task.ConfigureAwait(false);\n```\n\nA lightbulb code fix **\"Use ConfigureAwait(false)\"** is available in Visual Studio and VS Code to perform the replacement automatically.\n\n### AZC0012 — Avoid single-word type names\n\n```csharp\n// ❌ AZC0012 warning\npublic class Manager { }\n\n// ✅ Use a descriptive multi-word name\npublic class TableManager { }\n```\n\n### AZC0020 — Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext\n\n```csharp\n// ❌ AZC0020 warning — token is dropped\nawait client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext());\n\n// ✅ Propagate the token\nawait client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext { CancellationToken = cancellationToken });\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Suppressing a diagnostic\n\nMost rules can be suppressed with the standard `#pragma warning disable` / `#pragma warning restore` syntax or via a `[SuppressMessage]` attribute if the diagnostic is not configured as non-suppressible:\n\n```csharp\n#pragma warning disable AZC0012 // Justified: intentionally generic name used here\npublic class Format { }\n#pragma warning restore AZC0012\n```\n\n\u003e **Note:** AZC0101 is a **Warning** and can be suppressed. In the rare case where `ConfigureAwait(true)` is intentional, prefer omitting the `ConfigureAwait` call entirely.\n\n### Build output shows no diagnostics\n\nEnsure the package is referenced in your `.csproj` and that the project targets a framework compatible with Roslyn analyzers (net6.0 or later is recommended).\n\n## Next steps\n\n- Review the [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html)\n- See [docs/list-of-diagnostics.md](docs/list-of-diagnostics.md) for detailed per-rule documentation\n- Browse [AZC0101.md](docs/AZC0101.md) for detailed information on the ConfigureAwait rule\n\n## Contributing\n\nSee [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for contribution guidelines.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo test-backed snippets are required for this analyzer package — the examples above use plain `csharp` fences since there are no live client calls to exercise. If a snippet placeholder like `` ```C# Snippet:AZC0101Example `` is added to the README, a corresponding test file must be created:\n\n- **Test file path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/tests/Azure.SdkAnalyzers.Tests/Samples/Sample_AZC0101.cs`\n- Wrap sample code in `#region Snippet:AZC0101Example` / `#endregion`\n- Use `[Test]` (NUnit) on the method\n- Wrap test-only assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\n\nHowever, since these examples are of analyzer violations (not runnable code), it is acceptable to use plain `csharp` fences without snippet placeholders.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README must contain these sections in order. After applying Step 1b above, all are present:\n\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\n2. ✅ Key concepts\n3. ✅ Examples\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\n5. ✅ Next steps\n6. ✅ Contributing\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tools` — only needed if snippet placeholders were added to README\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tools` — only needed if public types/members were added or changed\n5. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[SdkAnalyzers] Docs: CHANGELOG missing AZC0101 entry and README missing standard sections"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136138Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136239Z **Package:** `Azure.SdkAnalyzers`
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136371Z **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136520Z **Triggered by:** commit f74b0b25c (PR #58154) by @m-redding
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136525Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136598Z ## What Changed
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136603Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1136681Z PR #58154 added:
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1137044Z - **AZC0101** analyzer: detects `ConfigureAwait(true)` in Azure SDK library code and warns to use `ConfigureAwait(false)` instead
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1137497Z - **ConfigureAwaitCodeFixProvider**: automatic code fixer that replaces `ConfigureAwait(true)` → `ConfigureAwait(false)`
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1137792Z - Shared async infrastructure: `AsyncPatternAnalyzer`, `MethodBodyAnalyzer`, `AsyncAnalyzerUtilities`
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1137797Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1137877Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1137881Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1138544Z - **CHANGELOG.md** lists only `AZC0012` in Features Added — does not mention the newly-added `AZC0101` rule, its code fixer, or the shared async pattern utilities introduced in this push
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1139150Z - **README.md** is missing the required top-level sections: *Getting started* (install, prerequisites, authenticate), *Key concepts*, *Examples*, *Troubleshooting*, *Next steps*, and *Contributing*
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1139620Z - **README.md** does not describe how library authors can suppress AZC0101 (or other rules), nor does it provide a quick usage example showing what the analyzer flags
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1139723Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1139960Z <details>
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140177Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140182Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140650Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140655Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140738Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140743Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140939Z #### 1a. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/CHANGELOG.md` — edit
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1140943Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141286Z Add an entry for AZC0101 and the code fixer under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`:
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141290Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141365Z ```markdown
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141441Z ### Features Added
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141445Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141547Z - Initial release of Azure.SdkAnalyzers
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141676Z - Added AZC0012: Avoid single word type names analyzer
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1141876Z - Added AZC0020: Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext analyzer
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1142185Z - Added AZC0101: Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` analyzer with automatic code fix (replaces `true` with `false`)
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1142256Z ```
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1142260Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1142443Z #### 1b. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` — edit
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1142447Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1143096Z Replace the current README content with a fully structured document matching the canonical template. The README currently has a valid intro and rules table; keep those and add the missing sections around them:
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1143101Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1143169Z ```markdown
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1143251Z # Azure SDK Analyzers
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1143255Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1143697Z Roslyn analyzers that enforce [Azure SDK Design Guidelines](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html) for .NET library authors.
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1143707Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144180Z This package is automatically included in all Azure SDK libraries in this repository to ensure consistent code quality and adherence to Azure SDK conventions.
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144185Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144258Z ## Getting started
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144261Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144344Z ### Install the package
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144354Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144520Z Install the `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` NuGet package via the .NET CLI:
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144524Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144596Z ```dotnetcli
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144692Z dotnet add package Azure.SdkAnalyzers
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144763Z ```
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144767Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144856Z Or via the NuGet Package Manager:
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144860Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1144937Z ```powershell
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145032Z Install-Package Azure.SdkAnalyzers
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145098Z ```
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145102Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145181Z ### Prerequisites
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145188Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145263Z - .NET SDK 6.0 or later
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145455Z - A project that targets any of the supported Azure SDK target frameworks
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145460Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145542Z ### Authenticate the client
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145546Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145767Z This package contains build-time analyzers only. No authentication is required.
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145772Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145844Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1145848Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1146814Z `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` provides Roslyn (compile-time) analyzers and code fixers that enforce patterns required by the [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html). Diagnostics fire as warnings during `dotnet build` and are visible in the IDE editor.
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1146818Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147067Z For the full list of rules, see [docs/list-of-diagnostics.md](docs/list-of-diagnostics.md).
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147071Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147287Z ## Implemented Rules
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147292Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147376Z | Rule | Description | Fix |
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147458Z |------|-------------|-----|
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147625Z | **AZC0012** | Avoid single word type names | — |
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1147846Z | **AZC0020** | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — |
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148092Z | **AZC0101** | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ |
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148097Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148174Z ## Examples
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148178Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148303Z ### AZC0101 — ConfigureAwait(false)
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148308Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148498Z The analyzer flags any `await` that passes `true` to `ConfigureAwait`:
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148503Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148586Z ```C# Snippet:AZC0101Example
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148869Z // ❌ AZC0101 warning — ConfigureAwait(true) is almost always wrong in library code
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148961Z await task.ConfigureAwait(true);
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1148973Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1149228Z // ✅ Correct — use false to avoid capturing the caller's SynchronizationContext
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1149318Z await task.ConfigureAwait(false);
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1149392Z ```
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1149396Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1149915Z A lightbulb code fix **"Use ConfigureAwait(false)"** is available in Visual Studio and VS Code to perform the replacement automatically.
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1149921Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150066Z ### AZC0012 — Avoid single-word type names
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150071Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150140Z ```csharp
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150243Z // ❌ AZC0012 warning
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150326Z public class Manager { }
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150330Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150461Z // ✅ Use a descriptive multi-word name
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150548Z public class TableManager { }
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150613Z ```
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150617Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150814Z ### AZC0020 — Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150826Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1150893Z ```csharp
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151022Z // ❌ AZC0020 warning — token is dropped
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151170Z await client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext());
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151175Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151286Z // ✅ Propagate the token
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151568Z await client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext { CancellationToken = cancellationToken });
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151639Z ```
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151642Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151717Z ## Troubleshooting
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151720Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151809Z ### Suppressing a diagnostic
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1151813Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152425Z Most rules can be suppressed with the standard `#pragma warning disable` / `#pragma warning restore` syntax or via a `[SuppressMessage]` attribute if the diagnostic is not configured as non-suppressible:
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152430Z 
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152502Z ```csharp
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152718Z #pragma warning disable AZC0012 // Justified: intentionally generic name used here
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152809Z public class Format { }
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152898Z #pragma warning restore AZC0012
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152967Z ```
2026-04-20T17:34:03.1152971Z
